They mistakenly believe the cost is dominated by the initial depthwise spatial filtering and calculate the reduction relative to t
realistic_solution: A depthwise separable convolution splits a standard convolution into two much cheaper steps: a 3x3 depthwise convolution (one filter per input channel) and a 1x1 pointwise convolution (to combine the channels). The computational savings are substantial.
napkin_math: The cost ratio is defined by (Cost_Depthwise + Cost_Pointwise) / Cost_Standard. This simplifies to the elegant formula: (1/Output_Channels) + (1/Kernel_Size^2).
